article.body_preview:
DHAKA - When people are already struggling to cope with high prices of daily essentials, a fresh hike in the prices of soybean oil, sugar, onions, and potatoes has dealt them a further blow.

This will only force them to dig deeper into their pockets at a time when inflation is close to double digits.

Crude soybean oil refiners on Wednesday raised the price of one-litre bottled soybean oil by Tk 12 to Tk 199 while the price of loose soybean oil was increased by Tk 9 to Tk 176 a litre. A consume

llm_connections.evidence_quote: Bangladesh Vegetable Oil Refiners' and Vanaspati Manufacturers' Association hiked the prices after consulting with the commerce ministry and the Bangladesh Trade and Tariff Commission.
llm_connections.scope_justification: The article explicitly describes a price hike by the vegetable oil refiners for soybean oil (a specific product), matching the 'Product' scope of a specific commercial item.

llm_connections.evidence_quote: the VAT on edible oil was reduced to 5 percent from 15 last year in the face of soaring prices of the cooking oil
llm_connections.scope_justification: The statement links soaring cooking-oil prices to a VAT reduction applied to edible oil, which concerns the specific product (edible/ cooking oil) described by the 'Product' feature.

llm_connections.evidence_quote: suppliers at the wholesale market told him that there has been a shortage in supply for about a month
llm_connections.scope_justification: The article reports suppliers and wholesale markets experiencing a month-long shortage, directly describing low available stock at supply/warehouse level, matching 'inventory_proxy' scope.
